behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so summaries can attribute statements to attendees.
behaviors.justification: A mock STT response can verify parsing, but it can miss real diarization/model failures (missing or unstable speaker labels). This behavior depends on real AI transcription output quality, so pure integration mocks can hide failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the real STT API, then verify transcript chunks include stable speaker labels and that downstream attribution maps to those labels in produced summary fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires a real STT model call; lower levels would not expose diarization failures.

behaviors.description: Summary includes at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: This is extraction/coverage quality from language understanding, not just schema wiring. Mocked LLM outputs would always satisfy the assertion if crafted, masking real misses where the model omits assignees.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real transcript with clearly assigned tasks to multiple attendees, call the real LLM summarizer, and assert each task-assigned attendee appears in action items at least once.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real model behavior is required; deterministic mocks would under-test the failure boundary.

behaviors.description: Key decisions from transcript appear in the dedicated "decisions" section, not only narrative text.
behaviors.justification: Section placement is prompt-following and semantic extraction behavior of the model. Mocking the model can hide real failures where decisions are present but misplaced or dropped.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript containing explicit decision statements, run real summarization, and assert those decisions are represented in the structured `decisions` field rather than only freeform narrative fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The check targets real LLM output structure/quality, so Agentic is necessary.

behaviors.description: Audio longer than 60 minutes is segmented, processed sequentially, and merged into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: The requirement explicitly spans multiple sequential processing steps with cross-step state and merged output. Real failures appear in step ordering, partial retries, and merge quality across multiple model/API calls.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, verify segment boundaries and sequential processing order, execute real STT+summarization per segment, then validate final merged summary is single structured output preserving entities/tasks/decisions across segments.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test exercises multi-step agentic orchestration; lower levels would miss cross-step merge/orchestration failures.

behaviors.description: End-to-end pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) runs successfully on a real meeting recording without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is full pipeline orchestration with real external services and multiple agentic stages. Mocking any major dependency can hide real operational and model-interaction failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like E2E test with a real recording and real dependencies: upload ingress, STT call(s), LLM summarization, and output delivery. Assert completion status, no human fallback path triggered, and valid structured result payload.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The description is explicitly end-to-end across multiple real agentic steps, matching Workflow.
